Tragacanth, also known as Tragacantha, is a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It is a natural source of gum that has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include mucilages, flavonoids, and triterpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include lozenges, syrups, and powders that can be used to treat coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
